tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'eyeglasses Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto's eyeglasses district could save $400+ on 4 pairs — where do locals actually shop?
tasks.reward: Locks in shopping stops for day 1-2 so you can buy early and wear them the rest of the trip
nuggets: Kyoto has optician clusters near Kawaramachi station
Most shops do same-day lens fitting
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan where I can buy 4 pairs during my trip (March 25-27 in Kyoto). I'm looking for affordable but quality frames and lenses. I want a list of 3-5 stores with their locations (Google Maps links), opening hours, price range estimates, and whether they typically serve English-speaking customers. Focus on areas near central Kyoto hotels (Kawaramachi/Gion area). Include any clusters or shopping districts known for eyeglasses.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance agent with subject 'Japan eyeglasses coverage'
tasks.hook_line: 2 days out — need to know if you can expense these or if it's cash only
tasks.reward: Clears the biggest unknown blocker for the 4-glasses plan
nuggets: US vision insurance rarely covers international
Need current prescription or can test in Japan
Some credit cards offer travel purchase protection
tasks.system_prompt: I'm traveling to Japan in 2 days and want to buy 4 pairs of eyeglasses there. Help me figure out: (1) whether my US vision insurance will reimburse international eyeglasses purchases and what documentation I need, (2) whether I need to bring my current prescription or if Japanese opticians can test and create a new one, (3) any other financial or legal considerations for buying multiple pairs abroad. I have standard US vision insurance through work.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and create 'Kyoto Must-See' saved list
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free, open 24hrs, best before 8am
Arashiyama bamboo grove magical before 9am crowds
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, nature, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, entry fees, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy spots. We're early risers and don't mind hiking. Also suggest 2-3 food stops per day (breakfast, lunch, dinner recommendations).

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Takayama day trips hiking March weather'
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is mountain town Japan — totally different vibe from Kyoto and Tokyo
tasks.reward: Locks in the mountain/nature break between temple cities
nuggets: Shirakawa-go UNESCO village 50min bus from Takayama
Old Town district walkable, traditional wooden houses
Late March still has snow on mountains
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 1.5 days in Takayama, Japan (March 28-29, 2026). Traveling with Dana. We want outdoor activities, hiking, mountain views, and traditional Japanese mountain town experience. Interested in day trips to nearby villages or nature spots. I want specific activity suggestions with timing, transportation details (bus/train schedules), and backup indoor options if weather is bad. Also recommend local food specialties to try (Hida beef, etc). We have moderate hiking experience.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Tokyo neighborhoods map'
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo has 23 wards — which neighborhoods match your vibe vs tourist traps?
tasks.reward: Completes all Japan trip planning — ready to just execute
nuggets: Shimokitazawa has vintage shops, indie cafes, young crowd
Tsukiji Outer Market still thriving for breakfast sushi
TeamLab Borderless requires advance tickets, sells out
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 5 days in Tokyo (March 30 - April 3, 2026). Traveling with Dana. We like exploring neighborhoods on foot, unique food experiences, art/design, shopping (fountain pens, watches, eyeglasses, stationery), and mixing tourist landmarks with local spots. Already planning to visit fountain pen stores (Itoya, Kingdom Note). I want a loose day-by-day framework organized by neighborhood, with 2-3 activities per day, food recommendations, and shopping stops. Include transportation tips (which subway lines). We stay up late and like nightlife.

tasks.first_domino: Open terminal and git clone the Cerebro repo
tasks.hook_line: Developer portal that won't run locally — can you untangle the setup?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all future Cerebro work and removes the 'parked' status
nuggets: Internal dev portal for engineering teams
Likely needs Docker, Node, database setup
Check README for environment variable requirements
tasks.system_prompt: I'm trying to get Cerebro (a developer portal for engineers at my company) running locally. I've cloned the repo but haven't gotten it working yet. Help me work through the setup process step by step. I need to understand: (1) what dependencies are required (Docker, Node version, databases, etc.), (2) how to configure environment variables and secrets, (3) common setup errors and how to debug them, (4) how to verify it's running correctly. Walk me through this methodically — I'm comfortable with dev tools but new to this specific codebase.

recommended_slots.reason: Weekend ramp-up energy matches low-effort research, and 2-day deadline makes this urgent
Medium-effort Kyoto planning unblocks rest of trip and batches well with slot 1
Quick 10-min errand clears blocker before Monday departure
